file_name: Microgenics Corporation - Collaborative Development and Commercialization Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 "Affiliate" shall mean, with respect to a Party, any corporation, or other business entity which directly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with that Party. A person or entity shall be regarded as in control of another entity if it owns, directly or indirectly, fifty percent (50%) or more of the outstanding equity securities of the subject entity which is entitled to vote in the election of directors, or a fifty percent (50%) or greater interest in the net assets or profits of the subject entity if such entity is not a corporation.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 "Affiliate" shall mean, with respect to a Party, any corporation, or other business entity which directly or indirectly has a business relationship with that Party. A person or entity shall be regarded as an affiliate of another entity if it is involved in collaborative projects, regardless of equity ownership.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Affiliate' specifies a clear ownership threshold (50% or more of equity securities). The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by broadening the definition to include entities merely having a business relationship or involved in collaborative projects, irrespective of ownership. This creates ambiguity as it's unclear whether sections relying on the original definition still apply, particularly concerning rights, obligations, and revenue sharing based on affiliate status, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2.3 Minimum Thresholds. In the event that, during the applicable Minimum Threshold Periods (as defined below), Microgenics does not receive the applicable Minimum Threshold Revenue (as defined below) during a given calendar year, Achaogen agrees to pay [***] Microgenics for such region during such calendar year (on a prorated basis, as applicable). For purposes of this Section 4.2.3, 4.2.3.1 The "First Minimum Threshold Period" shall begin on the [***] ([***]) anniversary of the date on which the First Commercial Sale of the Assay in [***] occurred and shall end on the [***] ([***]) anniversary of the date on which the First Commercial Sale of the Assay in [***] occurred. For example, if the First Commercial Sale of the Assay in [***] occurred on [***] then the First Minimum Threshold Period would begin on [***] and would end on [***]; 4.2.3.2 The "Second Minimum Threshold Period" shall begin on the [***] ([***]) anniversary of the earlier of the date on which the First Commercial Sale of the Assay occurred in (i) [***] of the following countries - [***] (each an "[***] Country"); or (ii) an [***] Country and [***]; or (iii) [***] ([***]) of the Primary Country list ([***]) (subclause (i), (ii), or (iii), as applicable, the "Start Date"), and shall end on the earlier of (a) the [***] ([***]) anniversary of the Start Date or (b) the date this Agreement expires or terminates; 4.2.3.3 "Minimum Threshold Revenue" shall mean, with respect to each of the First Minimum Threshold Period and the Second Minimum Threshold Period, USD $[***] of annual gross revenue received by Microgenics from sales of the Assay in the Territory (for clarity, during any overlap between the First Minimum Threshold Period and the Second Minimum Threshold Period, the total Minimum Threshold Revenue would be USD $[***]); provided, however, that the Minimum Threshold Revenue (i) shall be determined by [***] for a given Assay, less [***] directly associated with such sale and Assay and permitted to be taken in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles in the United States, and (ii) that in the event the Minimum Threshold Period begins or ends during a given calendar year, the Minimum Threshold Revenue for such calendar year shall be prorated accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Affiliate' based on a clear equity ownership threshold. The changed text broadens the definition to include entities merely having a business relationship or involved in collaborative projects, irrespective of ownership. This directly contradicts the application of Section 4.2.3 ('Minimum Threshold Revenue'), which relies on a clear definition of 'Affiliate' to determine revenue sharing and obligations. The broadened definition introduces ambiguity, making it unclear whether collaborative entities, without equity ownership, would be included in the minimum revenue calculations. This directly impacts the 'Minimum Threshold Revenue' calculation mentioned in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 7.2 Invoices; Mechanism of Payment. Upon the completion of each event as described in Section 7.1, Microgenics will invoice Achaogen within [***] ([***]) business days. Microgenics agrees to submit invoices to Achaogen (on a timely basis) for all payments due hereunder. Invoices shall reference Achaogen's contract number and the purchase order number. The invoices due under this Agreement shall be submitted to: Achaogen Inc. 7000 Shoreline Court, #371 South San Francisco, CA 94080 Reference: Achaogen Contract No. _____________, Attn.: _______ Email: All payments due Microgenics under this Agreement shall be made by Achaogen in United States dollars within [***] ([***]) days after receipt of the applicable invoice by ACH transfer to the credit and account of Microgenics at the following account: [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 7.2 Invoices; Mechanism of Payment. Upon the completion of each event as described in Section 7.1, Microgenics will invoice Achaogen within [***] ([***]) business days. Microgenics agrees to submit invoices to Achaogen (on a timely basis) for all payments due hereunder. Invoices shall reference Achaogen's contract number and the purchase order number. The invoices due under this Agreement shall be submitted to: Achaogen Inc. 7000 Shoreline Court, #371 South San Francisco, CA 94080 Reference: Achaogen Contract No. _____________, Attn.: _______ Email: All payments due Microgenics under this Agreement shall be made by Achaogen in United States dollars upon Achaogen's sole discretion after a 90-day review period of the invoice by ACH transfer to the credit and account of Microgenics at the following account: [***].
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that payments are due within [***] ([***]) days after receipt of the invoice. The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that payments are made upon Achaogen's sole discretion after a 90-day review period. This creates uncertainty regarding the payment timeline, undermining the original payment terms and could impact cash flow and financial planning for Microgenics.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.4.6 The termination, expiration or non-renewal of this Agreement shall not relieve either Party from any obligation that accrues pursuant to this Agreement before the effective date of the termination or expiration nor shall it release the Parties from any obligation that may have been incurred as a result of operations conducted under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 7.2 directly contradicts the principle established in Section 9.4.6. Section 9.4.6 states that termination or expiration of the agreement shall not relieve either party from obligations that accrue before the termination date. The original Section 7.2 defined a specific payment timeline ([***] days after invoice receipt). The modified version introduces a 'sole discretion' and '90-day review' period, which could delay or potentially negate payment obligations that have already accrued, thus contradicting Section 9.4.6. This introduces a strong in-text contradiction by potentially altering the obligations that are due before termination.

perturbation.original_text: 9.2 Termination At Will. Achaogen may terminate this Agreement in its entirety, for any reason, by providing at least sixty (60) days prior written notice to Microgenics.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.2 Termination At Will. Achaogen may terminate this Agreement in its entirety, for any reason, by providing at least sixty (60) days prior written notice to Microgenics. However, such termination is subject to a 180-day continuation clause to ensure project continuity.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants Achaogen the right to terminate at will with 60 days' notice. The modified text adds a '180-day continuation clause', creating an in-text contradiction. The termination is no longer truly 'at will' because a continuation clause limits Achaogen's immediate right to end the agreement. This affects the effective termination date and obligations during the continuation period, causing in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.4.5 If this Agreement is terminated during the Term at any time by Achaogen under Section 9.2 (Termination At Will) or by Microgenics under Section 9.3 (Termination for Cause), the following terms shall apply: 9.4.5.1 Solely to the extent the expiration or termination of this Agreement occurs prior to the payment of all development payments described in Section 7.1, Achaogen shall pay to Microgenics an amount equal to the first applicable unpaid development payment as described in Section 7.1 (Development Payments) for the period in which the Agreement is terminated or expires (for illustrative purposes only, if Achaogen provides notice of termination under Section 9.1 (Term) prior to the completion of the Phase 3: [***], then Achaogen shall pay an amount equal to USD $[***] to Microgenics pursuant to the terms of Section 7.2 (Invoices; Mechanism of Payment)); provided, that, notwithstanding the foregoing, no payment shall be due under this Section 9.4.5.1 in the event that this Agreement is terminated by Achaogen under Section 9.2 (Termination At Will) at any time in connection with the failure to obtain, or maintain, Regulatory Approval for Plazomicin; and 9.4.5.2 for a period of two (2) years after the expiration or termination date of this Agreement pursuant to Section 9.4.5, in the event Achaogen decides to continue to develop and commercialize Plazomicin, Achaogen shall provide written notice thereof to Microgenics and, upon Achaogen's receipt of a written proposal from Microgenics, the Parties shall use good faith efforts to negotiate a definitive agreement for the continued development, manufacture, supply and sale of the Assay by Microgenics on commercially reasonable terms; provided, however, that nothing in this Section 9.4.5.2 shall (a) obligate Achaogen to enter into any new agreement with Microgenics with respect to the development, manufacture, supply or sale of the Assay or (b) prohibit Achaogen from negotiating or entering into an agreement with any Third Party with respect to the development, manufacture, supply or sale of any assay.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows Achaogen to terminate the agreement 'at will' with 60 days' notice. The changed text adds a '180-day continuation clause'. This directly contradicts the 'at will' nature of the termination, creating an inconsistency regarding when the termination is effective and impacting obligations during the continuation period. Section 9.4.5 references termination under Section 9.2 (Termination At Will). The continuation clause now alters the understanding of Section 9.2, creating a contradiction that affects Section 9.4.5 and impacting the conditions laid out, therefore a strong contradiction exists.
